items.title: B.C. officials reveal estimated total costs of hosting FIFA World Cup
items.summary_auto: B.C. 's ministers of sport and economic growth give an update on the cost and revenue projections of hosting the FIFA World Cup in Vancouver.

items.title: ICE agent who faces charges in Minnesota shooting arrested in Texas
items.summary_auto: The top prosecutor from Minnesota's Hennepin County announced the arrest Friday of an ICE officer charged for shooting a Venezuela-born man in January at the height of a deadly immigration blitz launched by the Donald Trump administration.

items.title: South Bow targets 2027 decision on Keystone XL partial revival
items.summary_auto: Canada's South Bow will decide whether to proceed with its proposed partial revival of the Keystone XL oil pipeline by mid-2027, the company said on Friday, as it announced it has secured the shipper commitments it was seeking ​to advance the project.

items.title: Ottawa orders fresh look at Afghanistan heroism cases, opens door to Victoria Cross review
items.summary_auto: The federal government will create an independent military honours review board to reassess Afghanistan-era bravery cases, including whether Pte. Jess Larochelle merited the country's highest battlefield decoration.

items.title: Record-breaking heat and dry spring leave parts of England without water
items.summary_auto: Thousands of households in southeast England were left without water or facing low pressure during a ‌record-breaking heat wave this week, as high demand followed a dry spring to expose the failings in Britain's aging infrastructure.

items.title: Israeli PM says Lebanon ground offensive has expanded past key river
items.summary_auto: Israeli forces have advanced to positions north of Lebanon's Litani River, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Friday, as Israel escalates attacks against Hezbollah militants after warning thousands more Lebanese to flee their villages.

items.title: Poison seller Kenneth Law pleads guilty to abetting Ontario suicides, won’t be prosecuted for deaths in U.K.
Montreal urges 1.2 million residents to limit water use, warns of summer shortage
items.summary_auto: Montreal is asking people to reduce water use in a major way. Officials said new restrictive measures are necessary as it carries out repairs on a key water main.

items.title: Why was Gatineau's Habs watch party called offside?
items.summary_auto: Organizers had to cancel a watch party at Gatineau's Slush Puppie Centre where Montreal Canadiens fans had hoped to watch a broadcast of the team competing against the Carolina Hurricanes in the NHL's Eastern Conference final. The controversy made it all the way to Parliament Hill. CBC's Matthew Kupfer explains.

items.title: Poison seller Kenneth Law pleads guilty to abetting Ontario suicides, won't be prosecuted for deaths in U.K.
WHO chief arrives in Congo, says Ebola outbreak 'can be stopped'
items.summary_auto: Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, director-general of the World Health Organization, is in the Democratic Republic of Congo, he says, to show that communities affected by the Ebola outbreak 'are not alone,' and to ensure they have all they need to stop the spread.

items.title: Family 'devastated' by loss of Sheshatshiu, N.L., woman allegedly killed by partner
items.summary_auto: Beatrice Penashue has been identified as the woman who was killed in Sheshatshiu this week, in an alleged murder by her partner.

items.title: Ksi Lisims project would increase B.C.’s greenhouse gas emissions, calculations show
items.summary_auto: The Ksi Lisims LNG project, from which liquefied natural gas would be sold to a German utility under a deal announced Wednesday, would increase B.C. 's annual greenhouse gas emissions by between six and eight per cent, according to calculations by CBC News.

items.title: Canada slipped into a technical recession on an annualized basis as economic growth stalled in 1st quarter
items.summary_auto: Canada's economy ‌contracted in the first quarter of the year on an annualized basis by a slim margin, Statistics Canada data showed on Friday, making it two consecutive quarters of annualized decline, which some would call a technical recession.

items.title: Bishnoi gang sent letter to B.C. police force claiming 1,000 foot soldiers ready to carry out extortions
items.summary_auto: The Lawrence Bishnoi gang purportedly wrote police in Abbotsford, B.C., directly last summer claiming more than 1,000 foot soldiers were ready to carry out extortions, according to a police witness who testified at an immigration hearing Thursday.

items.title: Habs prepare for Game 5 down 3-1 to dominant Hurricanes
items.summary_auto: The Montreal Canadiens are fighting for their playoff lives tonight as they take on the Carolina Hurricanes in Game 5 of the NHL's Eastern Conference final.

items.title: Canadian manufacturer pivots to 'huge' market in India as the 2 countries try to strike trade deal
items.summary_auto: As India and Canada work toward a free trade deal, a Windsor, Ont., business that manufactures coatings for the automotive rubber industry is also eyeing the Indian market amid uncertainty linked to U.S. tariffs. “We need to be there," says the president of G. Bareich Import-Export Inc.

items.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes on launchpad at Cape Canaveral
items.summary_auto: An uncrewed Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on a Florida launchpad during a test on Thursday, in a major setback for Jeff Bezos’ space venture as it seeks to narrow the gap with Elon Musk's IPO-bound SpaceX.

items.title: NATO member Romania says Russian drone hit apartment building, injuring 2
items.summary_auto: NATO member Romania said on Friday that a drone injured two people in a southeastern city during an overnight Russian attack on neighbouring Ukraine, the first time in the war that a drone had hit a densely populated area in Romania and caused injuries.

items.title: MADD says N.L. impaired driving cases being dropped after trial delays 'not surprising'
items.summary_auto: CBC News has identified four impaired driving cases within the last 18 months that ended due to R v. Jordan — a 2016 Supreme Court of Canada decision that ensures an accused person has the right to be tried in a timely manner.

items.title: AI deepfakes of dozens of Canadian women in violent and sexual images shared online
What does hockey fandom look like in North Carolina?
items.summary_auto: Unlike Habs fans in Montreal — whose fandom is visibly widespread — Carolina Hurricanes fans, known as Caniacs, are a bit harder to spot in downtown Raleigh, N.C. In the parking lot of the Lenovo Center in the city's outskirts, it's a different story.

items.title: Generic Ozempic is now about $100 a month across the country. Canadians are taking note
items.summary_auto: Highly anticipated generic versions — which arrived at some pharmacies, including select Rexall and Shoppers Drug Mart locations across Canada last week — are being hailed as a gamer-changer. According to pharmacies, generics have slashed the price to roughly $100 a month, which they expect will quickly drive up demand.

items.title: World Cup matches in Toronto still aren't sold out. But fans are facing high costs
items.summary_auto: The World Cup is one of the most popular sporting events on the planet, but the demand hasn't been enough to sell out any of the six matches Toronto is hosting, with thousands of tickets still available for high prices.

items.title: Satan's Choice in new era of 'rapid recruitment' in Ontario, police say after Windsor-area arrest
items.summary_auto: A new era of the Satan's Choice outlaw motorcycle gang is picking up steam across Ontario as numbers grow, say police, adding that membership is fluid and hard to monitor.

items.title: Tenants on edge over dozens of fire code violations, unpaid bills at Thunder Bay, Ont., seniors' building
items.summary_auto: Residents of a seniors’ building in Thunder Bay, Ont., are weighing their options as their landlord faces unpaid utility bills and dozens of fire code violations. Formerly called McKellar Place, Vickers Manor, which is on sale for $18.9 million, is a privately owned six-storey building for those able to live independently. Here's why tenants are concerned about their future.

items.title: Got a World Cup ticket? Canadian officials warn: 'It's not a ticket into the country'
items.summary_auto: In just a few short weeks, Canada expects several thousand fervent soccer fans will arrive at our border. But the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) has a warning for fans. A coveted ticket may get you into the soccer stadium, but you have to get into Canada first.

items.title: Trump’s former attorney general questioned about Epstein Files
items.summary_auto: Donald Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi is appearing before the U.S. House oversight committee today — behind closed doors and not under oath — for a hearing about the Justice Department's handling of the Jeffrey Epstein case.

items.title: Not just bad for your lungs; air pollution damages your brain, McMaster University researchers say
items.summary_auto: A study out of Hamilton's McMaster University looked at how exposure to common air pollutants affected the performance of about 7,000 Canadians on tests of brain health.

items.title: 14-year-old Californian wins Scripps National Spelling Bee in lightning-round tiebreaker
items.summary_auto: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old from California, won a tense tiebreaker to take the title at this year's Scripps National Spelling Bee, becoming the best young speller in the English language.

items.title: First Nations say they're being disrespected as tension rises with Thunder Bay police over missing persons
items.summary_auto: Tensions are rising between First Nation leaders and police in Thunder Bay, Ont., following several recent high-profile missing persons cases in the city. Indigenous leaders say the role of searchers from their communities is being disregarded, while the local police chief has warned against the spread of "inaccurate or incomplete information."

items.title: Plastic surgeon ordered to pay $22.5M for filming patients with cameras inside Toronto clinic
8 grey whales have been found dead in B.C. this year
items.summary_auto: Researchers believe the whales are starving to death as they migrate back north to their northern feeding grounds.

items.title: Israeli and Russian forces added to UN's annual blacklist documenting sexual violence in conflict
items.summary_auto: An annual United Nations report documenting sexual violence in conflicts worldwide has included Israeli forces for the first time since the review began more than 15 years ago for their treatment of Palestinian detainees. Israel denies the accusations.

items.title: Crews recover bodies of 6 workers in wake of Washington state chemical-tank rupture
items.summary_auto: Crews have recovered six of nine workers missing and presumed to be dead after a chemical tank rupture in Washington state, officials said Thursday.

items.title: Central Alberta wildfire spurs evacuation order for part of County of Barrhead
items.summary_auto: A wildfire burning in County of Barrhead, northwest of Edmonton, triggered an evacuation order for some parts of the municipality on Thursday afternoon.

items.title: Lab meat: Will Canada get left behind?
items.summary_auto: Cultivated meat — real protein grown directly from animal cells — is officially moving out of the lab and into commercial production. While U.S. producers are scaling up, Canada is on a much more cautious timeline. CBC News’ Johanna Wagstaffe looks into the science behind cellular agriculture and the regulatory framework that novel foods must navigate before hitting Canadian shelves.

items.title: Israeli PM says he's directed military to take more of Gaza under its control
items.summary_auto: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Thursday he had directed Israel's military to take more of Gaza, initially by seizing ‌70 per cent of the Palestinian territory, where the population is already penned into a tiny strip of land along the coast.
